title: chenopodium rubrum subsp. botryoides
intro: Chenopodium rubrum subsp. botryoides, also known as red goosefoot or red fat-hen, is a subspecies of the Chenopodium rubrum plant, a type of flowering plant in the Amaranthaceae family. It is a perennial plant that is native to Europe and Western Asia, and has been used for its medicinal properties for centuries. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, and is used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, wounds, and skin conditions. The herbal preparations of Chenopodium rubrum subsp. botryoides include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
